conjecture_title: Open problem on equality $R_\ell(K_r, 2) = R(K_r, 2)$ for $r > 3$
summary: The question of whether the 2-colour list Ramsey number $R_\ell(K_r, 2)$ equals the ordinary Ramsey number $R(K_r, 2)$ for $r > 3$ remains entirely open. The base case $R_\ell(K_3, 2) = 6 = R(K_3, 2)$ is verified, but the source paper itself states that for larger complete graphs the problem is unresolved. No follow-up work settling this equality question in either direction was found in the indexed literature through May 2026.
search_queries: list Ramsey numbers complete graph equality 2-colour Alon Bucić 1902.07018
list Ramsey number K_r 2 colors equality ordinary Ramsey number
list Ramsey number cliques 2-colour equality progress 2022 2023 2024 2025
arxiv "list Ramsey" "R_ell" OR "list chromatic" complete graph 2-coloring equality 2021 2022 2023 2024 2025
notes: Four web searches and a fetch of the source abstract page returned no follow-up paper addressing $R_\ell(K_r,2) = R(K_r,2)$ for $r>3$. The source paper (published in J. Graph Theory 2021) itself states this is open. Recent Ramsey-theory breakthroughs (e.g., Campos--Griffiths--Morris--Sahasrabudhe 2023 on diagonal Ramsey, Mattheus--Verstraete 2023 on $R(4,t)$) concern ordinary Ramsey numbers and do not address the list variant. Status: open with high confidence.
